scope: Paper title 'Accretive Memory for Clinical AI: A Evidence-Bound Retrieval Substrate'
corrected_assertion: Paper title is reordered to 'Evidence-Bound Retrieval for Clinical AI: An Accretive Memory Substrate'. The new title leads with the principle layer (EBR) and treats the system name (Accretive Memory) as supporting context. Rationale: EBR is the spread-vehicle term for the paper — the coined acronym intended to sit alongside RAG / CRAG / RLHF / DPO / FLARE in citation indices. Leading with it in the title maximizes discoverability and term-adoption probability. Article 'A' → 'An' to match the vowel sound of 'Accretive'.
source: git commit d5c9f5a 'feat(EBR): rename accretive supersession → Evidence-Bound Retrieval (EBR)' 2026-05-27
